questions.context:
A bag contains 4 red counters, 3 blue counters and 5 green counters.
A counter is picked at random.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Work out the probability of picking a red counter.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Work out the probability of not picking a green counter.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: The bag contains only red, blue and green counters. Give a reason why the probabilities of the three colours must sum to 1.
parts.correctAnswer: These are all the possible outcomes, so their probabilities must sum to 1

markScheme.desc: They cover all possible outcomes / mutually exclusive and exhaustive oe

questions.context:
A fair spinner has 5 equal sections numbered 1 to 5.
The spinner is spun twice.

parts.label: a
parts.questionText:
Complete the tree diagram to show all possible outcomes for the two spins.
(First spin branches: 'odd' and 'even'. Second spin branches: 'odd' and 'even' from each.)

P(odd on one spin) = 3/5 and P(even on one spin) = 2/5.

markScheme.desc: First spin: P(odd)=3/5, P(even)=2/5 correctly placed
Second spin: both sets of branches show P(odd)=3/5, P(even)=2/5

parts.label: b
parts.questionText: Work out the probability that both spins land on odd numbers.

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Work out the probability that the two spins give different types (one odd and one even).

markScheme.desc: P(odd then even) + P(even then odd)

questions.context:
A school survey of 120 students records whether each student has a pet and whether they have a sibling.

| | Has a sibling | No sibling | Total |
|--|--------------|------------|-------|
| Has a pet     | 28 | 32 | 60 |
| No pet        | 36 | 24 | 60 |
| Total         | 64 | 56 | 120 |

parts.label: a
parts.questionText: Work out the probability that a randomly chosen student has both a pet and a sibling.

parts.label: b
parts.questionText:
A student is chosen at random from those who have a pet.
Work out the probability that this student has a sibling.
workingSteps: There are 60 students with a pet

parts.label: c
parts.questionText: Give a reason why having a pet and having a sibling appear to be independent in this data.
parts.correctAnswer: P(pet) = 60/120 = 1/2 and P(pet | sibling) = 28/64 ≈ 0.4375, which are not equal, so they are not independent

markScheme.desc: These are not equal, so having a pet and having a sibling are NOT independent

workingSteps: Since 0.5 ≠ 0.4375, pet and sibling are not independent
